chapter: Pompey the Great — From Sulla's Lieutenant to Rome's Master (79-62 BCE)
description: Easy Questions — Early Career, Lepidus, Sertorius, Spartacus

questions.question: Where was Pompey's family from, and what resource did this give him when he raised an army for Sulla in 83 BCE?
options: He was from Rome itself, where his family's senatorial connections allowed him to recruit from the legions stationed near the city
He was from Picenum in northeastern Italy, where his family's client networks and veterans gave him the manpower to raise three legions privately
He was from Capua in Campania, where his father's gladiatorial training school provided him with a ready-made fighting force
He was from Sicily, where his father had governed as praetor and left behind loyal communities that answered his call

questions.question: What nickname did Pompey's critics give him after his ruthless treatment of Marian survivors in Sicily?
options: Imperator
Felix
Adulescentulus carnifex
Magnus

questions.question: What did Pompey demand after his African campaign that was technically impossible under Roman law?
options: A second consulship before reaching the minimum age
A triumph, despite being a private citizen with no magistracy or legal command authority
Enrollment in the Senate without having held the quaestorship
Command of all armies in the western Mediterranean for an unlimited term

questions.question: What was Marcus Aemilius Lepidus's stated reason for marching his army toward Rome in 77 BCE?
options: He claimed Sulla had promised him a second consulship and the Senate was illegally blocking it
He declared himself the champion of those dispossessed by Sullan land confiscations and demanded a second consulship
He argued that Sulla's constitutional reforms were invalid because they had been passed under duress
He was responding to a popular petition from Rome's urban poor demanding the restoration of grain distributions

questions.question: What happened to Lepidus after his forces were defeated by Pompey and Catulus north of Rome?
options: He was captured and executed on Pompey's orders
He surrendered to the Senate and was sent into permanent exile in Greece
He retreated to Sardinia and died there, reportedly of illness and grief at his failure
He fled to Spain and joined Sertorius's rebel government

questions.question: What unusual institution did Sertorius establish in Spain to win the loyalty of the local population?
options: A network of Latin colonies modeled on those Rome had built throughout Italy
A Senate-in-exile of Roman refugees and a school at Osca educating Spanish nobles in Latin and Roman customs
A system of temples dedicated to Roman gods to introduce the Spanish population to Roman religion
A standing army funded by Spanish silver mines that he opened to free volunteers regardless of origin

questions.question: What ultimately ended the Sertorian War in 72 BCE?
options: Pompey defeated Sertorius in a decisive pitched battle near the Ebro River
Sertorius negotiated a surrender after Pompey cut off his supply lines from Mithridates
Sertorius was assassinated by his own lieutenant Marcus Perperna, who then lost to Pompey in open battle
Metellus Pius captured Sertorius's capital at Osca, forcing him to surrender

questions.question: Where did the slave revolt led by Spartacus begin in 73 BCE?
options: The silver mines of Sicily, where slaves had previously revolted twice before
A gladiatorial training facility at Capua in Campania
The grain estates of Apulia in southern Italy
The port of Brundisium, where slaves working the docks organized the breakout

questions.question: What ancient punishment did Crassus revive to restore discipline to his troops during the Slave War?
options: Fustuarium — beating a soldier to death with clubs, carried out by his own comrades
Decimation — the random execution of one soldier in ten from units that had performed dishonorably
Confiscation of pay and property for entire cohorts that had fled in battle
Reduction to the rank of slave laborer for any soldier who abandoned his post

questions.question: What did Crassus do to approximately six thousand captured slaves after the final defeat of Spartacus?
options: He sold them at public auction, using the proceeds to fund land grants for his veterans
He freed them conditionally, requiring them to serve in auxiliary units along Rome's northern frontier
He crucified them along the Appian Way from Capua to Rome as a deliberate warning
He transported them to Sicily to work the grain estates, replacing slaves killed during the revolt

questions.question: How did Pompey manage to claim credit for ending the Slave War despite Crassus doing most of the fighting?
options: He forged a letter from Crassus acknowledging Pompey's strategic leadership throughout the campaign
He arrived from Spain with his army, intercepted and destroyed the slave fugitives fleeing northward, then sent a dispatch to the Senate claiming he had pulled out the roots of the rebellion
He convinced the Senate that Crassus had acted illegally by using the punishment of decimation without proper authorization
He bribed members of the Senate to downplay Crassus's contribution in the official records of the campaign

questions.question: What lesser honor did the Senate award Crassus for defeating Spartacus, rather than a full triumph?
options: A supplicatio — a public day of thanksgiving voted by the Senate
An ovatio — a lesser form of triumph traditionally reserved for victories over slaves or pirates
A golden crown presented in the Senate chamber without a public procession
The title imperator without the accompanying triumph or procession
